test_detail: tests/B102-reingest-verify.ps1 -- 54 offline static + logic-mirror checks pass, 0 fail; 1 optional live-HTTP case SKIPs (run by setting $env:M8_BASE_URL post-deploy). Node is absent on host, so the gate is pure PS 5.1 like the rest of the suite.
dependency_check: Build-101 verified present BEFORE coding (commit 68317db 'Build-101: atomic ingest pipeline + re-extract existing sources' in log; api/ingest-extract-existing.js exists; reports/build-101-done.json exists). It landed via a parallel session mid-session after an initial pull showed it missing.
what_was_built: A deterministic 're-extract knowledge' chat command. classifyReextractKnowledge() in lib/intentClassifier.js (new INTENT.REEXTRACT_KNOWLEDGE + REEXTRACT_RE; triggers: re-extract / reextract / re extract, refresh ... knowledge graph, extract knowledge from (stored) sources; parses approve all|high). handleReextractKnowledgeCommand() in lib/orchestrator.js, wired into BOTH the buffered (log marker reextract_knowledge) and streaming (emit _rxS) dispatch paths, mirroring Build-100's driver-profile handler. It runs the same repair as POST /api/ingest-extract-existing: find sources with no node in m8_graph_nodes (source_doc_id), extractConcepts + populateGraph, return a plain-text summary.
deviation: The orchestrator handler drives the shared knowledge-intake lib directly (extractConcepts/populateGraph/savePendingNodes) instead of an HTTP self-call to /api/ingest-extract-existing. Reasons: (1) robust without a base URL inside the serverless runtime; (2) mirrors handleDriverProfileCommand, which drives cost-profiles directly; (3) editing the endpoint file was out of the allowed-edit scope (only intentClassifier.js + orchestrator.js). Same repair semantics + same response fields.
audit_finding: Re-extraction CANNOT add book knowledge with the current data, confirmed by live Supabase (project ltqpoupferwituusxwal) and matching the Build-101 audit. m8_knowledge_sources = 5 rows (ids 1,2,3,4,6), ALL tiny Collatz snippets 35-65 words; sources_with_book_meta = 0 (al-Bidaya wal-Nihaya / Ibn Kathir + Arktos were NEVER ingested, even at Step 1). m8_graph_nodes = 161 nodes, ALL math-engine kinds (conjecture/evidence/theorem/...), 0 book/source nodes; 20 nodes trace to ingested sources, so extractConcepts() (which DOES call Gemini, lib/knowledge-intake.js:165) is NOT broken -- it just has no book to extract. The only currently UNEXTRACTED source is #6, an accidental ingest of the Session-57 team brief. Running re-extract now would extract that junk, so it was deliberately NOT run live (nodes_added unknown-needs-live-run). ids 3 and 4 are exact duplicates.
real_fix: To make M8 actually 'know' the books, ingest the real book TEXT via the existing Build-78 ingest-book engine (lib/knowledge-intake.js ingestBookText / api/ingest-book.js / chat 'ingest ... book') -- it is resumable, checkpointed (m8_ingest_checkpoints table exists), and Arabic-chapter-aware. Only Muhammad can supply the book files. Recommended data hygiene first: delete the duplicate source (id 4) and the accidental team-brief source (id 6).
parallel_session_race: HEAD advanced repeatedly during this session (c140547 -> 66cd57e -> 8cda68b -> 122f2e1 ...) from a live parallel session. Staged ONLY my 3 files explicitly, never git add -A; working tree carried no foreign uncommitted edits at commit time.
notes: B102 code deliverable complete and green, but it is infrastructure: it adds no book knowledge until real books are ingested (see real_fix). The chat command is also a general repair tool -- it will extract any future source that was stored but not extracted (the exact failure mode Build-101's atomic pipeline prevents going forward).
